mappings.rationaleAI: Zheng He's voyages to East Africa (1405-1433) are directly relevant to B.1.a on medieval commercial contacts of Azania with Arabia, India, and the Mediterranean, as Ming Chinese trade reached the East African coast.
The Silk Road's maritime alternative routes connecting the Indian Ocean trade network are relevant to B.1.a on medieval commercial contacts between East Africa and Arabia/India.

The Berlin Conference (1884-85) and the Scramble for Africa provide the immediate context for C on East Africa from 1856 to c. 1900, including European exploratory activities and the colonial partition.

The Dutch East India Company's establishment of trading posts from the Cape to Japan is relevant to B.1.c on Portuguese invasions from 1502 and the subsequent Omani influence, as European commercial competition shaped East African coastal trade.
